data.title: Honeywell stock rises after earnings beat; raises 2025 guidance as spin-off nears
data.description:
Honeywell International (NASDAQ:HON) shares climbed 4.2% in premarket trading Thursday after the company reported third-quarter earnings that topped Wall Street’s expectations and raised its full-year outlook ahead of its planned Solstice Advanced Materials spin-off.

The industrial conglomerate reported net income of $1.9 billion, or $2.86 a share, up from $1.4 billion, or $2.17 a share, a year earlier -- topping Wall Street’s GAAP estimate of $2.37 a share. Adjusted earnings of $2.82 also exceeded analysts’ expectations of $2.57 a share.

Revenue totaled $10.4 billion, up 7% year over year and above the consensus estimate of $10.16 billion. Organic sales rose 6%, exceeding the high end of the company’s prior guidance, led by strong growth in its aerospace technologies and building automation units.

Operating income fell 6% to $1.8 billion, while segment profit rose 5% to $2.4 billion. Operating margin contracted 220 basis points to 16.9%, and segment margin slipped 50 basis points to 23.1%.

“As we progressed toward separating into three industry-leading public companies, we drove strong financial results and unlocked new value creation opportunities during the third quarter,” Chairman and Chief Executive Vimal Kapur said in a statement. “We are well positioned to continue building on our momentum and value creation efforts in the fourth quarter.”

Updated 2025 guidance

Honeywell (NASDAQ:HON) raised its full-year outlook to reflect stronger results and the upcoming October 30 spin-off of Solstice Advanced Materials.

  • Sales: $40.7 billion to $40.9 billion (about 6% organic growth)
  • Segment margin: 22.9% to 23.0% (up 30 to 40 basis points year over year)
  • Adjusted EPS: $10.60 to $10.70 (up $0.10 at the midpoint from prior guidance)
  • Operating cash flow: $6.4 billion to $6.8 billion
  • Free cash flow: $5.2 billion to $5.6 billion

The company said the spin-off of Solstice will reduce full-year sales by $0.7 billion and adjusted earnings by $0.21 a share.

Segment highlights

  • Aerospace Technologies: Sales up 12% organically, driven by 19% growth in commercial aftermarket and 10% in defense and space. Segment margin declined 160 basis points to 26.1%.
  • Industrial Automation: Organic sales rose 1% year over year, led by sensing products; segment margin contracted 150 basis points to 18.8%.
  • Building Automation: Organic sales grew 7% with strong demand in North America and the Middle East; segment margin expanded 80 basis points to 26.7%.
  • Energy and Sustainability Solutions: Organic sales fell 2%, with UOP sales down 13% offset by 5% growth in advanced materials.

Honeywell’s total backlog hit another record high, supported by a 22% increase in orders during the quarter. Operating cash flow surged 65% year over year to $3.3 billion, while free cash flow slipped 16% to $1.5 billion.

Portfolio transformation

Honeywell’s ongoing portfolio restructuring remains on schedule. The company is preparing to reorganize its automation business into three reporting segments beginning in 2026, following the spin-offs that will ultimately create three independent, publicly listed companies.

The spin of Solstice Advanced Materials remains set for completion on October 30, with further separations, including automation and aerospace, planned by the second half of 2026.

Kapur said Honeywell (HON) remains focused on “delivering outcomes-based solutions through Honeywell Forge and connected offerings,” as recurring revenue continues to grow across its digital and industrial platforms.

data.title: Churchill Downs unveils a new venue for its iconic racetrack in Louisville
data.description:
Churchill Downs Incorporated (NASDAQ:CHDN) announced a new growth project called Victory Run. The entertainment and gaming company said the project is centered on a new structure on the first turn of the Churchill Downs Racetrack between the First Turn Club and the Skye Terrace. Victory Run will replace uncovered, ground-level box seats and dated dining areas with a new premium hospitality and seating offering.

The Louisville-based company said the new four-story structure features various premium hospitality experiences with tremendous views of the finish line of the racetrack and will include private suites, indoor and outdoor dining, and covered box seating. The project will replace approximately 6,400 existing seats with premium seating for approximately 7,800 guests.

Pending incentives that must be approved first by the City of Louisville and then by the appropriate state agencies, including the Kentucky Cabinet for Economic Development, Churchill Downs (NASDAQ:CHDN) anticipates construction of Victory Run will begin following the 2026 Kentucky Derby and will be completed by the 2028 Kentucky Derby. The company plans to offer an interim upgraded seating experience in this area with temporary seating and enhanced amenities for the 2027 Kentucky Derby. 

Churchill Downs (CHDN) plans to spend $280 to $300 million on the project. 

"This project will create a greatly improved experience for our guests in an important section of our racetrack while delivering long-term growth and value for our shareholders," highlighted CEO Bill Carstanjen.

Weighing in on the development, Stifel analyst Jeffrey Stantial highlighted that the project originally had a wider scope and estimated cost of $455 million to $465 million. The firm reiterated its Buy rating on Churchill Downs (CHDN), calling it one of the best growth stories across gaming, reflecting a unique pipeline of high ROI expansion projects, synergy opportunities, and organic growth drivers.

Shares of Churchill Downs (CHDN) are up 1.8% following the company's third-quarter earnings update and Victory Run announcement.

data.title: Donnelley Financial completes pension plan termination, estimates ~$83M charge in Q3
data.description:
Donnelley Financial (NYSE:DFIN) announced on Thursday the completion of the termination of its primary defined benefit pension plan, and ~$83M charge in the third quarter related to the settlement.

The plan had been frozen since 2011.

The termination process had begun in 2024.

The company had settled the plan obligations through a combination of lump sum payments and the purchase of a non-participating irrevocable group annuity contract from a third-party insurer. Donnelley said it had made a cash contribution of $12.5M to fully fund the plan during the third quarter.

As a result, the company estimates a pre-tax non-cash settlement charge of ~$83M in its Q3 results.

The settlement results in the removal of a net liability of ~$10M from the company's balance sheet.

data.title: Enveric Biosciences to execute reverse stock split
data.description:
• Enveric Biosciences (NASDAQ:ENVB) plans to implement a reverse stock split of its common stock at a ratio of 1 post-split share for every 12 pre-split shares.
  • The reverse stock split will become effective at 8:00 a.m. ET on October 28, 2025. It will reduce the number of shares of Enveric’s common stock outstanding from 6,219,568 shares to ~518,297 shares.
  • ENVB --5.89% premarket to $0.63
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Freeport-McMoRan Non-GAAP EPS of $0.50 beats by $0.09, revenue of $6.97B beats by $240M
data.description:
• Freeport-McMoRan press release (NYSE:FCX): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.50 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $6.97B (+2.7% Y/Y) beats by $240M.
  • Consolidated production was impacted by the temporary suspension of operations in Indonesia since the September 8, 2025, mud rush incident.
  • Consolidated copper and gold sales were approximately 1% and 4%, respectively, lower than July 2025 guidance.
  • Consolidated unit net cash costs of $1.40 per pound of copper were better than July 2025 guidance.
  • • Consolidated production totaled 912 million pounds of copper, 287 thousand ounces of gold and 22 million pounds of molybdenum in third-quarter 2025.
  • Consolidated sales totaled 977 million pounds of copper, 336 thousand ounces of gold and 19 million pounds of molybdenum in third-quarter 2025.
  • Average realized prices were $4.68 per pound for copper, $3,539 per ounce for gold and $24.07 per pound for molybdenum in third-quarter 2025.
  • Average unit net cash costs were $1.40 per pound of copper in third-quarter 2025 and are expected to average $1.68 per pound of copper for the year 2025.
  • Operating cash flows totaled $1.7 billion, net of $0.2 billion of working capital and other uses, in third-quarter 2025.
  • Estimates for 2025 capital expenditures are approximately $0.4 billion below July 2025 guidance

data.title: Amerigo’s MVC signs three-year collective labor agreement
data.description:
• Amerigo Resources (TSX:ARG:CA) said on Thursday that it has signed a three-year collective labor agreement with the 210-member operators’ union of Minera Valle Central, the company's 100% owned operation located near Rancagua, Chile.
  • The agreement was reached after constructive negotiations. The new contract will be effective from October 29, 2025, and will continue through October 29, 2028.

data.title: Carpenter Technology reports mixed Q1 results; initiates Q2 outlook
data.description:
• Carpenter Technology press release (NYSE:CRS): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.43 beats by $0.27.
  • Revenue of $733.7M (+2.2% Y/Y) misses by $13.75M.
  • Delivered $153.3 million of adjusted operating income, up 31 percent year-over-year and a record quarterly result.
  • Bookings for Aerospace and Defense end-use market up 23 percent sequentially.
  • Completed negotiations on several long-term agreements with aerospace customers, with significant value realization.
  • Executed $49.1 million in share repurchases against $400.0 million repurchase program.
  • Outlook
    • For the second quarter of fiscal year 2026, anticipate between $152 million to $156 million in operating income
    • Expect fiscal year 2026 operating income to be in the range of $660 million to $700 million, representing a 26 percent to 33 percent increase over fiscal year 2025, with line of sight to the high end of the range from increased volume, pricing actions and productivity all contributing to higher profitability 
    • Project fiscal year 2026 adjusted free cash flow of $240 million to $280 million, net of $175 million to $185 million of spending related to the brownfield capacity expansion project
    • Committed to fiscal year 2027 target of $765 million to $800 million in operating income with opportunities to exceed
    • Well-positioned for continued growth beyond fiscal year 2027 with market dynamics continuing to strengthen and additional capacity coming online.

data.title: Jacobs expands role in strengthening Alaska’s maritime hub
data.description: • Jacobs (NYSE:J) has received a five-year contract extension to continue serving as program management consultant for the Don Young Port of Alaska (Port of Alaska) modernization program.

data.title: Stock index futures fall as investors assess Tesla results, U.S.-China tariff updates
data.description:
Stock index futures inched lower on Thursday, as investors assessed Tesla's (TSLA) earnings and the latest U.S.-China tariff updates, and awaited the September retail inflation numbers slated to come on Friday. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.2% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.2%.

Wall Street closed lower on Wednesday as investors digested Netflix’s (NFLX) latest earnings results.

"Markets struggled for momentum yesterday, with the S&P 500 (-0.53%) falling back after three consecutive gains. The main drivers were fears around the U.S.-China trade situation, weaker earnings announcements, as well as growing concerns about a protracted U.S. government shutdown," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said. 

Tesla (TSLA) was down 3% in premarket trade after releasing its Q3 earnings report as it warned on 'near-term uncertainty.' The firm also missed its profit estimates. 

On the tariffs side, the Trump administration was reportedly considering new restrictions on exports to China involving products made with U.S. software, escalating the ongoing technology and trade standoff between Washington and Beijing.

The September consumer price index report will be released on Friday, despite the ongoing U.S. government shutdown. The data is expected to show the health of the economy, especially before the Federal Reserve's monetary policy meeting next week.  

The U.S. government shutdown continued to drag on and became the second-longest in history, as lawmakers remained deadlocked over expiring health-care subsidies.

"Indeed, the Polymarket probabilities currently suggest there’s a 75% chance that this will be the longest shutdown in history, so it could be some time before the regular flow of U.S. economic data resumes," Allen said. 

Earnings for the day include Intel (INTC), Ford Motor Company (NYSE:F), Nokia (NOK),  American Airlines Group (NASDAQ:AAL), Dow Chemical (NYSE:DOW), and Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV), among others.
